data.content:
There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone. The backtrace contains only:

| Frame | Library | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| #00 | `libc.so` | Android bionic C library |
| #01 | `libutils.so` | Android system utilities |
| #02 | `libnativeapp.so` | App's own native (C/C++) library |
| #03–#05 | `libart.so` | Android Runtime (Java/Kotlin ART) |

The app (`com.example.nativeapp`) appears to be a pure native/Java Android app — there's no `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, or any other .NET library present. Nothing here to symbolicate on the .NET side.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see the backtrace frames. The user asked me to "symbolicate the .NET runtime frames." Let me analyze what's in the trace:

1. `libc.so` - Android system library (bionic C library)
2. `libutils.so` - Android system library
3. `libnativeapp.so` - A native app library (not .NET)
4. `libart.so` - Android Runtime (ART) library (3 frames)

There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone. The libraries present are:
- `libc.so` - Android bionic


- `libutils.so` - Android utilities
- `libnativeapp.so` - The app's native library
- `libart.so` - Android Runtime (Java/Kotlin ART, not .NET)

The app `com.example.nativeapp` appears to be a pure native Android application without any .NET runtime components like `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, or `libmonodroid.so`.
